candidates.title: Stability-Optimal Weight Decay: A Lyapunov Control Framework Unifying Dynamic WD Methods
candidates.summary: Unified framework grounded in Lyapunov stability theory and PMP that characterizes all WD methods as special cases of an optimal control problem. Derives alignment-aware WD from first principles. Proves budget equivalence under low AIS, explaining the empirical null result. Proposes Phi-Modulator Taxonomy, three standardized metrics (BEM, CSI, AIS), and controller taxonomy.
hypotheses: H1: Phi-Modulator Taxonomy with unified steady-state formula r* = gamma*E[||g||cos(theta)] / (lambda*E[Phi_t])
H2: Lyapunov alignment cross-term elimination via lambda_t proportional to (1-delta_t)
H3: Budget equivalence under AIS < 0.5 explains null result
H4: PMP derivation recovers alignment-aware WD as optimal control
H5: AIS architecture dependence (BN vs non-BN)
H6: SWD instability explained by high CSI
H7: Hill function cooperativity for noise robustness
candidates.pilot_focus: Verify steady-state formula prediction accuracy (H1) and CSI reduction under alignment-aware WD (H2) on ResNet-20/CIFAR-10 with 3 seeds
key_strengths: Combines theoretical depth (4 theorems) with empirical rigor (pre-registered null tests)
Explains BOTH when dynamic WD works and when it fails
Multiple layers of novelty: taxonomy, PMP derivation, AIS threshold, controller mapping
Directly builds on established results (Sun et al. CVPR 2025, Defazio 2025, CWD ICLR 2026)
key_risks: Theoretical scope may be too broad for one paper
PMP costate identification p ~ -g_t may be too crude
AIS threshold may not cleanly separate regimes

candidates.title: A Feedback Control Theory of Weight Decay: Unifying Dynamic WD Methods via Gradient-to-Weight Ratio Control
candidates.summary: Frame all WD methods as feedback controllers (P, PI, PD, sliding-mode, setpoint) targeting R_t = ||g_t||/||w_t||. Design RAWD optimizer with stability-derived gain bound. More conceptually accessible than PMP/Lyapunov but less theoretically deep.
hypotheses: All WD methods are controllers targeting the same plant output R_t
RAWD P-controller achieves lower CSI than all existing methods
CSI metric predicts generalization gap across WD methods
candidates.pilot_focus: Track R_t trajectories for 5 WD methods, confirm convergence to similar R* setpoint
key_strengths: Immediately prescriptive: best controller for your training dynamics
RAWD implementable in <10 lines of PyTorch
key_risks: Closed-loop stability analysis is as hard as convergence proofs
R_t plant model only well-characterized for normalized layers

candidates.title: When Does Alignment-Aware Weight Decay Fail? A Systematic Null-Hypothesis Evaluation
candidates.summary: Primarily empirical: systematic null-hypothesis test with pre-registered falsification criteria, BEM-controlled comparisons, n=9 seeds. AIS threshold provides the theoretical explanation for the null result. Safest option with highest empirical rigor.
hypotheses: H3: Budget equivalence under low AIS
No dynamic WD beats constant WD on CIFAR/ImageNet at n=9 seeds
candidates.pilot_focus: Extend iter_003 results to VGG-16-BN and increase to 9 seeds
key_strengths: Already partially validated by iter_003 data
Publishable as positive or negative result

selection_rationale: cand_lyapunov_control is selected as front-runner because it uniquely combines theoretical depth (PMP + Lyapunov proofs) with the empirical null-result explanation (AIS threshold), addressing the strongest elements from all 6 perspectives. The innovator's controller framing is incorporated as the CSI/controller-taxonomy component. The pragmatist's architecture generalization and empiricist's null-hypothesis testing are the experimental backbone. The contrarian's scale-invariance concern is addressed via the BN vs non-BN ablation. The interdisciplinary PMP derivation provides the deepest theoretical novelty. The theoretical perspective's Lyapunov variance analysis is the central proof.
